data.title: Bank of Hawaii outlines potential 2.90% NIM by end of 2026 as deposit remix drives margin expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Bank of Hawaii Corporation (BOH) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Peter Ho highlighted, "We recorded yet another set of strong results in the fourth quarter. Fully diluted earnings per share was $1.39 per share, 63% higher than results from a year ago and 16% higher than last quarter." He noted that net interest margin improved for the seventh straight quarter, reaching 2.61%, and return on common equity improved to 15%. Ho emphasized, "Loans and deposits both grew modestly in the quarter. Importantly, noninterest-bearing demand deposits grew 6.6% on a linked basis. Credit quality remained and remains pristine."
  • The CEO stated that Bank of Hawaii continues to advance its dominant market position, with market share growth in 2025 advancing another 40 basis points, reinforcing its deposit market share leadership in Hawaii. Interest-bearing deposit costs improved by 20 basis points, and the company remixed $659 million in fixed rate loans and investments from a roll-off rate of 4% into a roll-on rate of 5.8%, supporting the net interest margin.
  • Ho remarked, "We believe NIM by the end of 2026 could come in near the 2.90% range."
  • Vice Chair & Chief Risk Officer S. Shairson reported, "Our portfolio is built on long-tenured relationships with approximately 60% of both our commercial and consumer clients having been with the bank for more than 10 years." He added that "credit quality in these segments also remained strong with average FICO scores of 730 for auto loans and 761 for personal loans."
  • Vice Chair & CFO Bradley Satenberg stated, "For the quarter, we reported net income of $60.9 million and a diluted EPS of $1.39, an increase of $7.6 million and $0.19 per share compared to the linked quarter. These increases were primarily due to the continued expansion of our net interest income and our net interest margin."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated that Q4 marked the seventh consecutive quarter of net interest margin expansion and stated, "We believe NIM by the end of 2026 could come in near the 2.90% range." Satenberg expects "another solid improvement in the cost of our deposits during the first quarter," and projects first quarter normalized noninterest income between $42 million and $43 million.
  • Satenberg forecasted expenses to "increase by between 3% and 3.5% from our 2025 normalized expenses" for 2026, with first quarter noninterest expense approximately $113 million.

Financial Results

  • Net income for the quarter was $60.9 million, with diluted EPS of $1.39. Net interest margin rose to 2.61%. Noninterest income was $44.3 million, which included an $18.1 million gain on the sale of the Merchant Services portfolio and a $16.8 million loss from repositioning the investment portfolio. Noninterest expense was $109.5 million.
  • Satenberg highlighted, "This is the first time since the second quarter of 2022 after the Fed started raising rates that the mix shift had a positive impact on our earnings."
  • The allowance for credit losses on loans and leases ended the quarter at $146.8 million, with a coverage ratio of 1.04%.
  • The company paid dividends of $28 million on common stock and $5.3 million on preferreds, and repurchased approximately $5 million of common shares at an average price of $65 per share, with plans to increase repurchases next quarter.

Q&A

  • Matthew Clark, Piper Sandler: Asked about the sustainability of noninterest-bearing deposit growth. Ho responded, "The fourth quarter might be a bit outsized...we've seen growth in that category for a few quarters now...we would anticipate this probably continuing...but probably not at that same clip of 6-plus percent."
  • Clark inquired about loan growth pipeline. President Polk said, "Until we can get both consumer and commercial kind of both contributing to growth, I think we'd probably stick in the mid-single -- in the low single digits at this point."
  • Jeff Rulis, D.A. Davidson: Asked for clarification on the margin target. Ho confirmed, "That's the way we're thinking about it...end of year, not a fourth quarter average of 2.90%."
  • Rulis sought information on special mention and classified balances. Shairson replied, "Special mention at the end of the fourth quarter was $63.4 million...total classified at $298.5 million."
  • Jared David Shaw, Barclays: Asked about market share gains. Ho stated, "I like to believe that our historic performance is an indicator of what's possible for the future...I just -- I don't really see a condition that would lead me to believe that, that's going to retard at all often in the future."
  • Kelly Motta, KBW: Requested insight on fee income potential. Polk shared, "You can look at quarter-over-quarter, we had a little over 2% growth in fees on a linked quarter basis...getting into that 10% range...I think we feel pretty reasonable about that."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts demonstrated a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on sustainability of recent gains and clarity on pipeline growth, but also probing on margin sensitivity, loan growth, and capital deployment. Questions sought verification and detail, rather than expressing concern or pressing for defensive answers.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone, especially in prepared remarks, with Ho and Satenberg emphasizing achievements and positive outlooks. In Q&A, the tone remained constructive, occasionally clarifying details but not displaying defensiveness or hesitation.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts exhibited increased confidence, with less focus on risk or headwinds and more attention on growth opportunities and improved metrics.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language became more specific this quarter, with management projecting a potential 2.90% NIM by year-end 2026, compared to more generalized NIM improvement commentary last quarter.
  • Strategic focus continues to be margin expansion, credit quality, and market share leadership. Wealth management momentum was referenced again, with fee income guidance reiterating a 10% potential growth range.
  • Analysts' questions shifted from risk and portfolio derisking to the sustainability of margin and deposit gains, and capital return.
  • Key metrics improved: EPS rose from $1.20 to $1.39, NIM expanded from 2.46% to 2.61%, and credit quality remained strong. Management and analyst sentiment both became more optimistic, with fewer concerns about noncore exposures or expense management.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited modest increases in delinquencies and criticized loans, though overall credit metrics remained strong. Shairson noted, "Delinquencies increased to 36 basis points...criticized loans increased to 2.12% of total loans. Notably, 86% of criticized assets are real estate secured with a weighted average LTV of 54%."
  • Analysts probed on the sustainability of deposit growth, margin expansion, and loan growth, but did not express major concerns about underlying business risks.

Final Takeaway

Bank of Hawaii management presented a quarter of strong performance, highlighted by ongoing net interest margin expansion and robust credit quality. Projected NIM for year-end 2026 was set near 2.90%, supported by deposit remix and disciplined balance sheet management. Loan and deposit growth remained modest, while fee income initiatives in wealth management continued to gain traction. Capital deployment was active, with dividends and the resumption of share repurchases, as management expressed confidence in the current strategy, balance sheet stability, and future earnings momentum.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Magnum Ice Cream rallies to a post-Unilever separation high
data.description:
The Magnum Ice Cream Company N.V. (MICC) broke to its highest trading level since the company made its public debut on December 8 on the Euronext Amsterdam, the London Stock Exchange, and the New York Stock Exchange.

Amsterdam-based The Magnum Ice Cream Company (MICC) was formed via Unilever's (UL) 2025 ice cream demerger. It owns leading global brands including Magnum, Ben & Jerry’s, Cornetto, Wall’s, Talenti, Popsicle, and others, with around 21% share of the global ice cream market and multi-billion-euro annual revenue. The business traces its heritage back over a century through legacy brands such as Breyers and Wall's.

During the company's Capital Markets Day last September, Magnum Ice Cream (MICC) management said it expects above-market growth, consistent margin expansion, and improving free cash flow as a standalone company. Looking ahead, the plan is for a balanced capital allocation strategy focused on delivering organic growth, productivity, and cash.

Shares of Magnum Ice Cream (MICC) rallied 8.0% on Monday and swapped hands as high as $18.38. The forward price-to-earnings ratio for new buyers of the stock is 13.9X.

data.title: Airbus Helicopters sees order surge on European defense demand
data.description:
Airbus Helicopters reported a sharp increase in new business last year, with orders climbing by nearly 20% as rising defense budgets across Europe boosted demand for military aircraft.

The helicopter unit of Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) recorded 544 gross orders in 2025, or 536 on a net basis after cancellations, according to figures released Monday. That compares with 455 gross orders and 450 net orders the previous year.

Aircraft deliveries also moved higher, increasing 8.5% to 392 helicopters, up from 361 in 2024.

Chief Executive Bruno Even, who is set to leave the role in April after seven years at the helm, said the company is operating in an environment where attention to defense and security priorities is at its strongest in decades.

Military demand played a major role in the year’s performance, including a December agreement with Spain for 100 military helicopters.

Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) said its share of the global military helicopter market expanded to 28% in 2025, while its civil helicopter market share declined to 51% from 57% a year earlier.

Detailed financial results for Airbus Helicopters will be released alongside the group’s full-year earnings on February 19.

data.title: Adnoc raises stake in NextDecade's Rio Grande LNG project in Texas
data.description:
Abu Dhabi state oil company Adnoc said Monday it has increased its stake in the Rio Grande liquefied natural gas project in Texas, buying a 7.6% equity interest in the second phase of the project from BlackRock's (BLK) Global Infrastructure Partners.

Adnoc did not disclose the acquisition price for the stake in Phase 2, which will include two trains capable of processing ~12M metric tons/year of LNG per year and costing more than $13B, according to developer NextDecade (NEXT).

The deal follows Adnoc's 2024 purchase of an 11.7% stake in the initial phase of Rio Grande LNG, which when all phases are complete, is expected to be one of the world's largest LNG facilities with capacity to ship as much as 60M metric tons/year of LNG.

Rio Grande LNG is being developed by NextDecade (NEXT) with financial backing from GIP, Adnoc's overseas investment arm XRG, TotalEnergies (TTE) and several other partners; construction on Phase 1 of the project in Brownsville, Texas, began in 2023 and is expected to begin production in 2027, and Phase 2 is likely to be substantially complete by 2031.

NextDecade (NEXT) said recently it has initiated the pre-filing process with the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission for expansion at Rio Grande LNG that includes a sixth liquefaction train and an additional marine berth.

data.title: LKQ may be worth low-to-mid $50s/share in takeout - analyst
data.description:
LKQ Corp. (LKQ) may be worth low-to-mid $50s/share in a potential takeover after the company said it's exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale. Shares of LKQ ticked higher by 0.4%.

The theoretical sum-of-the-parts analysis is based off of 2025 financial expectations, including NA Wholesale - $5.7bn - $6.1bn (~7x multiple),  Europe - $3.0bn - $3.4bn (5x-6x) and Specialty - $500mn - $700mn (5x-6x), Roth analyst Scott Stember wrote in a note on Monday. 

"While current headwinds in the North American Wholesale and European segments have placed the near- to mid-term profit prospects in question, a theoretical exercise ofvaluing the company on its individual parts could lead one to conclude that there is significant longer-term value to be unleashed," Stember, who has a buy rating and $43 a share price target, wrote in the note. 

LKQ (LKQ) also said it's continuing with the previously announced process to explore the potential sale of its specialty segment.

"When analyzing each segment separately and assigning an appropriate multiple, we can't help but conclude that the shares would be worth significantly more than the current ~$34 quote, or rough an EV of $12.5bn+," Stember wrote. "That said, our SOTP analysis suggests that LKQ could actually be worth more than $13bn (also factoring in debt of $4.2bn and offsetting cash of $289mn, as of Q3), for a total EV north of $13bn, or in the low- to mid-$50 per share range."

data.title: BlackRock files for new bitcoin ETF with covered-call strategy
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) has filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission to launch an exchange-traded fund that offers bitcoin (BTC-USD) exposure while generating income by selling options.

The iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT), which BlackRock (BLK) launched in early 2024 and now dominates the spot BTC ETF arena, simply tracks the price of bitcoin (BTC-USD). By comparison, the proposed iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF would also try to generate additional income by selling call options mainly on IBIT, in what's known as a covered-call strategy.

The new fund does not yet have a ticker, and no fee has been defined.

Among the tradable bitcoin-linked (BTC-USD) funds that offer similar strategies for options-based income generation are the Roundhill Bitcoin Covered Call Strategy ETF (YBTC), as well as the Amplify Bitcoin Max Income Covered Call ETF (BAGY) and the NEOS Bitcoin High Income ETF (BTCI).

data.title: Silver should be trading at half its price later this year – JPM’s Marko Kolanovic
data.description:
Silver (XAGUSD:CUR) should be trading at half its current price later in 2026, said Marko Kolanovic, former chief strategist and co-head of Global Research at J. P. Morgan.

The precious metal reached a top of $113.25 per ounce on Monday before shooting back to around $104-$110. But, according to Kolanovic, this is the result of “meme traders attempting to take over the market.”

Both silver (XAGUSD:CUR) and gold (XAUUSD:CUR) are currently rallying as political tensions rise, and predictions markets are now foreseeing an 80% probability that a partial federal government shutdown could occur by the end of this week.

Silver (XAGUSD:CUR) has advanced about 54% in January so far, an impressive 264% from a year ago.

However, these prices “will not last—silver should trade at [about] half these levels later this year,” said Kolanovic.

data.title: Eldorado Gold upgraded at Scotiabank with Skouries project poised to enter production
data.description:
Eldorado Gold (EGO) +6.7% in Monday's trading, off earlier highs in reaching an all-time high $50.67, after gold prices rose to all-time highs above $5,100/oz and Scotiabank upgraded shares to Outperform from Sector Perform with a $59 price target, raised from $33, saying sharply higher gold and silver price forecasts materially improve the company’s outlook.

As gold prices soar past $5,000/oz, Eldorado Gold (EGO) is poised to gain, especially with the timing of new projects coming online, Scotiabank analyst Tanya Jakusconek said, expecting gold to average ~$4,600 in 2026 and 2027, well above the bank's earlier estimates, with prices staying higher through the decade.

"The upgrade for EGO is mainly due to the Skouries project [in Greece] entering production in 1Q '26," Jakusconek wrote. "We expect the stock to re-rate as the company moves to operating from development - moving from negative FCF to positive FCF - with the mine adding growth."

As metals continue their meteoric rise, Scotiabank narrowed down its list of outsized winners after many companies released their production, cost, and capital expenditure guidance for 2026, seeing Eldorado (EGO), Pan American Silver (PAAS), and Endeavour Mining (EDVMF) as the group's top free cash flow generators this year.

The bank expects these companies will produce the most free cash flow this year with robust yields, with Eldorado (EGO) at 18%, Pan American (PAAS) at ~12%, and Endeavour Mining (EDVMF) at ~8%.

data.title: Micron dips after a six-session rally
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) shares fell 1.99% to $391.70 in afternoon trade on Monday, after reports said Samsung is nearing Nvidia certification for its HBM4 AI memory chips, raising competitive pressure in the high-bandwidth memory segment.

The stock had surged about 18.72% between January 15 and January 23, far outpacing the S&P 500’s 0.42% decline over the same period.

The pullback followed a report that Samsung is nearing Nvidia certification for its HBM4 AI memory chips, intensifying competition in high-bandwidth memory used in Nvidia’s AI accelerators. The report also said Samsung and SK hynix are looking to raise server memory prices by up to 70% as surging AI demand strains global supply, even as Samsung denied rumors of an 80% price hike across its memory products.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, Micron is rated Strong Buy with a score of 4.99 out of 5, with grades of A+ in growth, profitability, and momentum, a B+ in revisions, and a B in valuation.

Seeking Alpha analysts also suggest buying the stock.

Turning to Wall Street, 37 out of 43 analysts rate the stock Buy or higher, four rate it Hold, and two rate it Strong Sell.

An analyst said MU remains a buy as it is positioned in a “super-upcycle driven by AI demand across data centers, mobile, and industrial markets,” noting that Q1’26 revenue surged 57% year over year and that its high-bandwidth memory volumes for 2026 are already sold out. The analyst added that Micron plans to raise fiscal 2026 CapEx to about $20B to expand HBM and DRAM supply, and said a forward PEG of 0.24 indicates the stock is undervalued despite recent gains.

The stock remains up 38.23% year-to-date and has inched 37.35% higher over the past month.

data.title: Anthropic asked Apple for 'several billion dollars' per year for Siri deal: report
data.description:
• Apple (AAPL) may have chosen Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) to help run the next version of its Siri voice assistant, but the iPhone maker had also spoken with Anthropic (ANTRHO), Bloomberg reported.
  • Apple was in discussions with both Anthropic and OpenAI (OPENAI) about helping run Siri and handle parts of Apple Intelligence, the news outlet reported. However, Anthropic, which makes the popular Claude family of artificial intelligence models, wanted “several billion dollars annually over multiple years” for the efforts, the news outlet added. Apple eventually decided those terms were not favorable and moved past Anthropic.
  • Earlier this month, it was reported that OpenAI had made a “conscious decision” to sit out the aforementioned deal with Apple, given that the ChatGPT maker and Apple are increasingly becoming competitors. 
  • The Apple-Google deal is a multi-year partnership, and Apple is believed to be paying Google billions of dollars over the life of the deal.
  • Apple shares were up modestly in late afternoon trading on Monday.
